---
title: "BonQuery"
subtitle: "Toronto Shelter System: An Independent Data Audit."
description: "BonQuery turns humanitarian open data into clear, useful analyses — starting with Toronto's shelter system capacity, demand, and flow. Built for journalists, researchers, advocates, policymakers, and anyone paying attention."
author: "Miriam Marling"
open-graph:
  title: "BonQuery — Data for the Greater Good"
  description: "BonQuery turns humanitarian open data into clear, useful analyses — starting with Toronto's shelter system capacity, demand, and flow."
  site_name: "BonQuery — Data for the Greater Good"
twitter-card:
  card-style: summary
  title: "BonQuery — Data for the Greater Good"
  description: "BonQuery turns humanitarian open data into clear, useful analyses — starting with Toronto's shelter system capacity, demand, and flow."
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
daily_occ    = FileAttachment("data/daily_occupancy.json").json()
bt_data      = FileAttachment("data/city_bridging_triage.json").json()
shelter_flow = FileAttachment("data/shelter_flow.json").json()
seasonRug    = FileAttachment("data/season_rug.csv").csv().then(rows =>
  rows.map(d => ({ min_temp: +d.min_temp, winter_season: d.winter_season }))
)
binnedSC     = FileAttachment("data/cold_response_binned_by_winter_sc.csv").csv().then(rows =>
  rows.map(d => ({
    min_temp_bin:  +d.min_temp_bin,
    winter_season: d.winter_season,
    mean_unplaced: +d.mean_unplaced,
    n:             +d.n
  }))
)
```

```{=html}
<style>
.quarto-container, .container-fluid, .container { max-width: none !important; }
</style>
```

## Winter Accountability Monitor

::: {.bq-alert .bq-alert-hero}
**WINTER ACCOUNTABILITY MONITOR: 2026–2027.** The City of Toronto
[activates its cold-weather response](https://www.toronto.ca/community-people/housing-shelter/homeless-help/warming-centres/){target="_blank"}
when temperatures reach −5 °C. Its own data shows what
follows: across six winters, at least one person or couple was still unplaced at
the 4 a.m. closeout on 257 of 268 nights at or below −5 °C (95.9%). A couple
counts once in these figures, so the number of people left outside is higher
still. Our standard is simple: no one left unplaced. Daily monitoring resumes
when the 2026–2027 cold season opens in November.
:::

::: {.bq-cta}
[View the Cold-Weather Evidence →](projects/cold-response.qmd){.btn .btn-danger .btn-lg
role="button"}

[About the Data: How to Read These Numbers →](projects/about-the-data.qmd){.btn
.btn-outline-danger .btn-lg role="button"}
:::

::: {.key-finding-box}

```{=html}
<div class="key-finding-box-header">COLD-WEATHER ACCOUNTABILITY</div>
```

### Singles and couples left unplaced on freezing nights — six winters of evidence

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
cs      = getComputedStyle(document.body)
refLine = cs.getPropertyValue("--bq-chart-stroke").trim()

seasonColorMap = new Map([
  ["2020-21", "#4DF884"],
  ["2021-22", "#2F9DF5"],
  ["2022-23", "#DEDD32"],
  ["2023-24", "#E63946"],
  ["2024-25", "#F65F18"],
  ["2025-26", "#900C00"]
])
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selectedWinters = {
  const allSeasons = [
    "2020-21","2021-22","2022-23","2023-24","2024-25","2025-26"
  ];
  const selected = new Set(allSeasons);

  if (!document.getElementById("bq-season-cb-style")) {
    const s = document.createElement("style");
    s.id = "bq-season-cb-style";
    s.textContent = `
      .bq-season-cb {
        appearance: none;
        -webkit-appearance: none;
        width: 14px;
        height: 14px;
        border-radius: 2px;
        margin: 0;
        border: 1.5px solid #888;
        cursor: pointer;
        display: inline-flex;
        align-items: center;
        justify-content: center;
        flex-shrink: 0;
        background: transparent;
      }
      .bq-season-cb:checked {
        background-color: var(--season-color);
        border-color: var(--season-color);
      }
      .bq-season-cb:checked::after {
        content: "✓";
        color: #fff;
        font-size: 10px;
        font-weight: 700;
        line-height: 1;
      }
      .bq-season-cb:hover {
        transform: scale(1.15);
        box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.15);
      }
      .bq-season-lbl {
        display: inline-flex;
        align-items: center;
        gap: 8px;
        cursor: pointer;
        user-select: none;
      }
    `;
    document.head.appendChild(s);
  }

  const root = document.createElement("div");
  root.style.cssText = "font-family:var(--sans-serif,sans-serif);font-size:13.5px;";

  const labelEl = document.createElement("div");
  labelEl.textContent = "Seasons shown";
  labelEl.style.cssText = "font-weight:600;margin-bottom:3px;";

  const hint = document.createElement("div");
  hint.textContent = "Click a season to show or hide it";
  hint.style.cssText =
    "font-size:11.5px;font-weight:400;color:var(--bq-fg-faint);margin-bottom:6px;";

  const row = document.createElement("div");
  row.style.cssText =
    "display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:6px 20px;align-items:center;";

  for (const season of allSeasons) {
    const color = seasonColorMap.get(season);

    const cb = document.createElement("input");
    cb.type = "checkbox"; cb.value = season; cb.checked = true;
    cb.className = "bq-season-cb";
    cb.style.setProperty("--season-color", color);

    const txt = document.createElement("span");
    txt.textContent = season;

    const lbl = document.createElement("label");
    lbl.className = "bq-season-lbl";
    lbl.append(cb, txt);
    row.append(lbl);

    cb.addEventListener("change", () => {
      if (cb.checked) selected.add(season); else selected.delete(season);
      root.value = [...selected];
      root.dispatchEvent(new Event("input"));
    });
  }

  root.append(labelEl, hint, row);
  root.value = allSeasons;
  return root;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
scSeasons = selectedWinters
```

:::{.callout-note collapse="false"}
**Reading the chart.** The chart shows data from up to six cold seasons
(November 15 to April 15), colour-coded by winter year. The vertical lines
across the background act as a temperature rug — each line marks one night's
recorded overnight minimum temperature, coloured to match its season. Where
these lines cluster together, data is plentiful; where they thin out (below
about −15 °C), individual nights carry more weight. Use the checkboxes above
to show or hide individual seasons.
:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const visibleBinned = binnedSC.filter(d => scSeasons.includes(d.winter_season))
  const visibleRug    = seasonRug.filter(d => scSeasons.includes(d.winter_season))

  return Plot.plot({
    width:        720,
    height:       400,
    marginLeft:   70,
    marginRight:  20,
    marginTop:    20,
    marginBottom: 50,
    color: {
      type:   "ordinal",
      legend: true,
      label:  "Winter season",
      domain: [...seasonColorMap.keys()],
      range:  [...seasonColorMap.values()]
    },
    r: { range: [2, 8] },
    x: {
      label: "Overnight minimum temperature (°C)",
      labelAnchor: "center",
      reverse: true
    },
    y: {
      label: "Mean singles & couples unplaced (observed)",
      labelAnchor: "top",
      grid: true
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.tickX(visibleRug, {
        x: "min_temp",
        stroke: "winter_season",
        frameAnchor: "bottom",
        strokeOpacity: 0.35,
        strokeWidth: 1
      }),
      Plot.ruleX([0, -5, -10, -15], {
        stroke: refLine,
        strokeWidth: 1,
        strokeDasharray: "4,3"
      }),
      Plot.dot(visibleBinned, {
        x: "min_temp_bin",
        y: "mean_unplaced",
        fill: "winter_season",
        r: "n"
      }),
      Plot.tip(visibleBinned, Plot.pointer({
        x: "min_temp_bin",
        y: "mean_unplaced",
        title: d =>
          `${d.winter_season}\n` +
          `${d.min_temp_bin} °C bin  ·  ${d.n} nights\n` +
          `Observed mean: ${d.mean_unplaced.toFixed(1)} singles/couples unplaced`
      }))
    ]
  })
}
```

*Figure 1: Observed mean number of singles and couples unplaced at 4 a.m. by
overnight minimum temperature and cold season, binned in 2 °C intervals. A
couple is counted once in the City's data, so each unit represents one or two
people and the number of people affected is at least this high. Bins are
labelled by their midpoint (e.g., the −3 °C dot covers −4 °C up to but not
including −2 °C). Dot size scales with the number of nights in that bin.*

[See the full analysis: Left in the Cold →](projects/cold-response.qmd){.btn .btn-danger .btn-lg role="button"}

:::

## The data we're tracking

<!-- SYNC: both callout boxes below must be kept in sync with projects/toronto-shelter.qmd.
     Paths here are adjusted for root-level rendering (projects/ prefix added). -->

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Monthly Shelter System Flow

### The dashboards

::: {.mobile-dashboard-links}
Open each dashboard directly in your browser — they're built for
interactive use and work best full-screen on a phone.

- [Monthly Snapshot →](projects/monthly-snapshot.html)
- [Year-to-Date Comparison →](projects/ytd-comparison.html)
- [Historical Trends →](projects/historical-trends.html)
:::

::: {.desktop-dashboard-tabs}

The three tabs below show different views of the data, with interactive
filters on every chart.

::: {.panel-tabset}

#### Monthly Snapshot

A point-in-time view of the shelter system for any month from January
2018 forward. Use the date filter at the top to change months.

[Open in a new tab →](projects/monthly-snapshot.html){target="_blank"}

<iframe
  src="projects/monthly-snapshot.html"
  width="100%"
  height="2800"
  class="bq-embed"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

#### Year-to-Date Comparison

Compares the current year's cumulative figures against the same period
the prior year. Useful for seeing whether the system is on track to
exceed, match, or fall below last year.

[Open in a new tab →](projects/ytd-comparison.html){target="_blank"}

<iframe
  src="projects/ytd-comparison.html"
  width="100%"
  height="900"
  class="bq-embed"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

#### Historical Trends

Multi-year time series for the headline metrics, with filters for
population group, year, and month. Useful for spotting long-term
patterns or seasonal trends.

[Open in a new tab →](projects/historical-trends.html){target="_blank"}

<iframe
  src="projects/historical-trends.html"
  width="100%"
  height="3800"
  class="bq-embed"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

:::

:::

```{=html}
<style>
.mobile-dashboard-links { display: none !important; }
.desktop-dashboard-tabs { display: block; }
@media (max-width: 768px) {
  .desktop-dashboard-tabs iframe {
    width: 100vw !important;
    margin-left: calc(50% - 50vw) !important;
    border: none !important;
    border-radius: 0 !important;
  }
}
</style>
```

:::

---

::: {.callout-note .bq-green collapse="true" title="Daily Occupancy & Capacity"}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// ── Date utilities ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function fmtDate(s) {
  const [y, m, d] = s.split("-").map(Number)
  return new Date(y, m - 1, d).toLocaleDateString("en-US", {
    year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric"
  })
}

allDates = [...new Set(daily_occ.map(r => r.date))].sort().reverse()
latestDate = allDates[0]
// cityValidation = FileAttachment("data/city_validation.json").json().catch(() => null)
// ^ kept commented while the validation banner is hidden; re-enable if we surface
//   reconciled City vs BonQuery comparisons after the capacity analysis lands.
```

BonQuery's version of this table adds two features the City's page doesn't have. The **date selector** below lets you browse any date from January 2021 to the present; the City's [Daily Shelter & Overnight Service Usage page](https://www.toronto.ca/city-government/data-research-maps/research-reports/housing-and-homelessness-research-and-reports/shelter-census/){target="_blank"} shows only its most recently published date. The **Offline** column (calculated as `funded − actual`) flags beds, rooms, or spaces temporarily out of service — see the [data validation page](projects/data-validation.html){target="_blank"} for what these are and why they matter.

## Daily Occupancy & Capacity for ${fmtDate(selectedDate)}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// ── Pipeline validation (CKAN-to-CKAN) — runs on most recent date ─────────
// These checks confirm BonQuery's aggregation pipeline is internally correct.
// They do not compare against the City's live dashboard.

latestRows = daily_occ.filter(r => r.date === latestDate)

function getVal(rows, key) {
  const row = rows.find(r => r.key === key)
  return row ? row.ind : null
}

validationChecks = {
  const rows = latestRows
  const checks = []

  // Check 1: all expected keys present
  const expectedKeys = [
    "all_shelter","room_based","singles_sector","singles_shelter","allied_summ",
    "temp_summ","iso_summ","fam_total","fam_emerg","fam_trans_r","fam_hotel",
    "sng_hotel","singles_total","emerg_total","mix_emerg","men_emerg","wom_emerg",
    "yth_emerg","trans_total","mix_trans","fam_trans_b","men_trans","wom_trans",
    "yth_trans","allied_total","respites","dropin","temp_resp","hotels","iso"
  ]
  const presentKeys = new Set(rows.map(r => r.key))
  const missingKeys = expectedKeys.filter(k => !presentKeys.has(k))
  checks.push({
    name: "All expected rows present",
    pass: missingKeys.length === 0,
    detail: missingKeys.length > 0 ? `Missing: ${missingKeys.join(", ")}` : null
  })

  // Check 2: All Shelter Programs total = sum of components
  const allShelter = getVal(rows, "all_shelter")
  const compSum = ["fam_total","sng_hotel","singles_total","allied_total","temp_resp","hotels","iso"]
    .reduce((s, k) => s + (getVal(rows, k) ?? 0), 0)
  checks.push({
    name: "All Shelter Programs rollup matches components",
    pass: allShelter === compSum,
    detail: allShelter !== compSum
      ? `all_shelter.ind=${allShelter}, component sum=${compSum}, diff=${allShelter - compSum}`
      : null
  })

  // Check 3: Singles sectors total = emergency + transitional
  const singlesTotal = getVal(rows, "singles_total")
  const emergTotal = getVal(rows, "emerg_total")
  const transTotal = getVal(rows, "trans_total")
  checks.push({
    name: "Singles sectors rollup: emergency + transitional = singles total",
    pass: singlesTotal === (emergTotal + transTotal),
    detail: singlesTotal !== (emergTotal + transTotal)
      ? `singles=${singlesTotal}, emerg+trans=${emergTotal + transTotal}`
      : null
  })

  // Check 4: For every row with non-null cap, occ + unocc = cap (within 1, for rounding)
  const capRows = rows.filter(r => r.cap !== null && r.cap > 0)
  const arithFails = capRows.filter(r => Math.abs(r.occ + r.unocc - r.cap) > 0.5)
  checks.push({
    name: "Internal arithmetic: occupied + unoccupied = actual capacity",
    pass: arithFails.length === 0,
    detail: arithFails.length > 0
      ? arithFails.map(r => `${r.key}: ${r.occ}+${r.unocc}≠${r.cap}`).join("; ")
      : null
  })

  return checks
}

allPass = validationChecks.every(c => c.pass)

// ── Validation banner ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
validationBanner = {
  const CITY_CENSUS_URL = "https://www.toronto.ca/city-government/data-research-maps/research-reports/housing-and-homelessness-research-and-reports/shelter-census/"

  if (!allPass) {
    const failedChecks = validationChecks.filter(c => !c.pass)
    return html`<div style="
      background:var(--bq-warn-bg);border:1px solid var(--bq-warn-border);border-radius:4px;
      padding:8px 14px;font-size:0.88em;color:var(--bq-warn-fg);margin-bottom:12px">
      <strong>⚠ Validation issue detected for ${fmtDate(latestDate)}</strong>
      <details style="margin-top:6px">
        <summary style="cursor:pointer">Show details</summary>
        <ul style="margin:6px 0 0;padding-left:20px">
          ${failedChecks.map(c => html`<li>${c.name}${c.detail ? html` — <em>${c.detail}</em>` : ""}</li>`)}
        </ul>
      </details>
    </div>`
  }

  // ── City-vs-BonQuery banner temporarily hidden ───────────────────────────
  // Bridging & Triage (29 ppl) and known capacity differences cause the
  // current validator to always fail. Re-enable after the capacity
  // reconciliation analysis so we can show a clean ✓ / ≈ / ✗ status that
  // distinguishes documented structural differences from genuine discrepancies.
  //
  // if (cityValidation === null) {
  //   return html`<div style="
  //     background:#e9ecef;border:1px solid #adb5bd;border-radius:4px;
  //     padding:8px 14px;font-size:0.88em;color:#495057;margin-bottom:12px">
  //     Validation status unavailable in local development
  //   </div>`
  // }
  // if (!cityValidation.city_reachable) {
  //   return html`<div style="
  //     background:#fff3cd;border:1px solid #e0a800;border-radius:4px;
  //     padding:8px 14px;font-size:0.88em;color:#533f03;margin-bottom:12px">
  //     ⚠ City of Toronto shelter census page could not be reached · <a href="${CITY_CENSUS_URL}" target="_blank">View City data →</a>
  //   </div>`
  // }
  // if (cityValidation.passed && cityValidation.city_date === cityValidation.bonquery_latest_date) {
  //   return html`<div style="
  //     background:#d4edda;border:1px solid #76b88d;border-radius:4px;
  //     padding:8px 14px;font-size:0.88em;color:#1a4731;margin-bottom:12px">
  //     ✓ Validated against <a href="${CITY_CENSUS_URL}" target="_blank">City's Daily Shelter &amp; Overnight Service Usage page</a> for ${fmtDate(cityValidation.city_date)}
  //   </div>`
  // }
  // if (cityValidation.passed && cityValidation.city_date !== cityValidation.bonquery_latest_date) {
  //   return html`<div style="
  //     background:#d4edda;border:1px solid #76b88d;border-radius:4px;
  //     padding:8px 14px;font-size:0.88em;color:#1a4731;margin-bottom:12px">
  //     ✓ Last validated against <a href="${CITY_CENSUS_URL}" target="_blank">City's Daily Shelter &amp; Overnight Service Usage page</a> for ${fmtDate(cityValidation.city_date)} · BonQuery data current to ${fmtDate(cityValidation.bonquery_latest_date)}.
  //   </div>`
  // }
  // return html`<div style="
  //   background:#fff3cd;border:1px solid #e0a800;border-radius:4px;
  //   padding:8px 14px;font-size:0.88em;color:#533f03;margin-bottom:12px">
  //   ⚠ Discrepancy found for ${fmtDate(cityValidation.city_date)} — BonQuery values differ from <a href="${CITY_CENSUS_URL}" target="_blank">City's published table</a>
  // </div>`

  return html``
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selectedDate = Inputs.select(allDates, {
  label: "Date",
  format: d => fmtDate(d)
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<p class="bq-ckan-note">Latest available from City of Toronto Open Data: <strong>${fmtDate(latestDate)}</strong>.</p>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// ── Table rendering ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dayRows = daily_occ.filter(r => r.date === selectedDate)

function n(v) { return v !== null && v !== undefined ? v.toLocaleString() : "—" }
function pct(v) { return v !== null && v !== undefined ? v.toFixed(1) + "%" : "—" }

function offlineCell(r) {
  if (r.cap === null || r.cap === undefined) return `<td></td>`
  if (r.cap_funding === null || r.cap_funding === undefined) return `<td></td>`
  const offline = r.cap_funding - r.cap
  if (offline <= 0) {
    return `<td class="bq-num">${offline.toLocaleString()}</td>`
  }
  return `<td class="bq-num bq-danger">${offline.toLocaleString()}</td>`
}

function tableRow(r, col1Label) {
  const pad = (r.indent ?? 0) * 16
  const cls = r.is_total ? "bq-row-total" : "bq-row"
  const bld = r.is_total ? " bq-bold" : ""
  if (r.col_type === "summary") {
    return `<tr class="${cls}">
      <td style="padding-left:${pad}px" class="${bld}">${r.label}</td>
      <td class="bq-num${bld}">${n(r.ind)}</td>
      <td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td>
    </tr>`
  }
  return `<tr class="${cls}">
    <td style="padding-left:${pad}px" class="${bld}">${r.label}</td>
    <td class="bq-num${bld}">${n(r.ind)}</td>
    <td class="bq-num">${n(r.occ)}</td>
    <td class="bq-num">${n(r.unocc)}</td>
    <td class="bq-num">${n(r.cap)}</td>
    ${offlineCell(r)}
    <td class="bq-num">${pct(r.rate)}</td>
  </tr>`
}

function sectionHeader(label, col1, col2, col3, col4) {
  return `<tr class="bq-th">
    <td>${label}</td>
    <td class="bq-num">Individuals</td>
    <td class="bq-num">${col1}</td>
    <td class="bq-num">${col2}</td>
    <td class="bq-num">${col3}</td>
    <td class="bq-num">${col4}</td>
    <td class="bq-num">Occupancy Rate</td>
  </tr>`
}

function summaryHeader() {
  return `<tr class="bq-th">
    <td>Occupancy Summary</td>
    <td class="bq-num">Individuals</td>
    <td colspan="5"></td>
  </tr>`
}

dailyTable = {
  const s = (key) => dayRows.find(r => r.key === key)

  const allShelterInd = s("all_shelter") ? s("all_shelter").ind : null
  const btEntry  = bt_data && bt_data.entries ? bt_data.entries[selectedDate] : null
  const btInd    = btEntry ? btEntry.bridging_triage : null
  const btStart  = bt_data && bt_data.first_captured
    ? bt_data.first_captured
    : "the date scraping began"
  const totalInd = (allShelterInd !== null && btInd !== null)
    ? allShelterInd + btInd
    : allShelterInd

  const totalAccomRow = `<tr class="bq-row-total bq-bold">
    <td>Total People Accommodated †</td>
    <td class="bq-num">${n(totalInd)}</td>
    <td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td>
  </tr>`
  const bridgingRow = btInd !== null
    ? `<tr class="bq-row-alt">
        <td style="padding-left:26px">Bridging &amp; Triage Programs</td>
        <td class="bq-num">${n(btInd)}</td>
        <td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td>
       </tr>`
    : `<tr class="bq-row-na">
        <td style="padding-left:26px">Bridging &amp; Triage Programs</td>
        <td class="bq-num" style="font-size:0.85em"
          >Not available for this date</td>
        <td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td>
       </tr>`

  const summaryKeys = ["all_shelter","room_based","singles_sector","singles_shelter","allied_summ","temp_summ","iso_summ"]
  const roomKeys    = ["fam_total","fam_emerg","fam_trans_r","fam_hotel","sng_hotel"]
  const bedKeys     = ["singles_total","emerg_total","mix_emerg","men_emerg","wom_emerg","yth_emerg",
                       "trans_total","mix_trans","fam_trans_b","men_trans","wom_trans","yth_trans"]
  const alliedKeys  = ["allied_total","respites","dropin"]

  return html`
    <div style="overflow-x:auto;margin:0 0 1rem">
    <table class="bq-table">
      ${summaryHeader()}
      ${totalAccomRow}
      ${bridgingRow}
      ${summaryKeys.map(k => s(k) ? tableRow(s(k)) : "").join("")}

      ${sectionHeader("Shelter Programs, Room-Based", "Occupied Rooms", "Unoccupied Rooms", "Actual Room Capacity", "Offline Rooms")}
      ${roomKeys.map(k => s(k) ? tableRow(s(k)) : "").join("")}

      ${sectionHeader("Shelter Programs, Bed-Based", "Occupied Beds", "Unoccupied Beds", "Actual Bed Capacity", "Offline Beds")}
      ${bedKeys.map(k => s(k) ? tableRow(s(k)) : "").join("")}

      ${sectionHeader("Allied Services", "Occupied Spaces", "Unoccupied Spaces", "Actual Space Capacity", "Offline Spaces")}
      ${alliedKeys.map(k => s(k) ? tableRow(s(k)) : "").join("")}

      <tr class="bq-th">
        <td>Temporary Programs, Bed/Space-Based</td>
        <td class="bq-num">Individuals</td>
        <td class="bq-num">Occupied Beds/Spaces</td>
        <td class="bq-num">Unoccupied</td>
        <td class="bq-num">Actual Capacity</td>
        <td class="bq-num">Offline Beds/Spaces</td>
        <td class="bq-num">Occupancy Rate</td>
      </tr>
      ${s("temp_resp") ? tableRow(s("temp_resp")) : ""}

      ${sectionHeader("Temporary Programs, Room/Unit-Based", "Occupied Rooms", "Unoccupied Rooms", "Actual Room Capacity", "Offline Rooms")}
      ${s("hotels") ? tableRow(s("hotels")) : ""}

      ${sectionHeader("Temporary Isolation/Recovery Programs", "Occupied Rooms", "Unoccupied Rooms", "Actual Room Capacity", "Offline Rooms")}
      ${s("iso") ? tableRow(s("iso")) : ""}
    </table>
    <p class="bq-footnote">
      † Total People Accommodated = All Shelter Programs + Bridging &amp; Triage Programs.
      Bridging &amp; Triage figures are not published to the City of Toronto open data (CKAN)
      export. BonQuery scrapes them daily from the City's shelter census page when the City
      posts the table; data is available from ${btStart} onward.
      On days when the City has not yet published (weekends, holidays, or publication delays),
      the Bridging &amp; Triage row will show "Not available for this date" and the Total
      will reflect All Shelter Programs only.
    </p>
    </div>`
}
```

This table reproduces the City of Toronto's Daily Shelter & Overnight Service Usage report using the City's open data. The **All Shelter Programs** subtotal and all breakdown rows are derived from the CKAN open data export. **Bridging & Triage Programs** are tracked in the City's operational system but not published to open data; BonQuery scrapes this figure daily from the City's shelter census page and displays it where available. The **Total People Accommodated** figure equals All Shelter Programs + Bridging & Triage when both are available. Some capacity and unoccupied-bed figures may differ slightly from the City's live dashboard due to offline-bed reporting differences between the CKAN export and the City's operational system. A full side-by-side comparison is available on the [data validation page](projects/data-validation.html){target="_blank"}.

Contains information licensed under the [Open Government Licence – Toronto](https://open.toronto.ca/open-data-licence/). Source: City of Toronto Open Data — [Daily Shelter & Overnight Service Occupancy & Capacity](https://open.toronto.ca/dataset/daily-shelter-overnight-service-occupancy-capacity/). Aggregated by [BonQuery](https://bonquery.ca).

BonQuery's aggregated data is available for download from 2021 to present: [JSON](https://bonquery.ca/data/daily_occupancy.json) · [CSV](https://bonquery.ca/data/daily_occupancy.csv)

:::

---

::: {.callout-note .bq-purple collapse="true"}
## Shelter System Requests for Referrals

Monthly average daily calls to Toronto's 24/7 shelter referral line — calls referred
to a shelter space, unmatched individual callers, and total calls handled — with
interactive date controls. Replicates and extends the City's
[Shelter System Requests for Referrals](https://www.toronto.ca/city-government/data-research-maps/research-reports/housing-and-homelessness-research-and-reports/shelter-system-requests-for-referrals/)
page. Data sourced from two separate CKAN resources (call wrap-up codes and service
queue); see the page for important caveats on combining the two series.

::: {.mobile-dashboard-links}
- [Shelter System Requests for Referrals →](projects/central-intake.html)
:::

::: {.desktop-dashboard-tabs}

[Open in a new tab →](projects/central-intake.html){target="_blank"}

<iframe
  src="projects/central-intake.html"
  width="100%"
  height="4200"
  class="bq-embed"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

:::

Contains information licensed under the [Open Government Licence – Toronto](https://open.toronto.ca/open-data-licence/). Source: City of Toronto Open Data — [Central Intake Calls](https://open.toronto.ca/dataset/central-intake-calls/). Analysis and charts by BonQuery.
:::

<!-- END SYNC -->

---

*BonQuery is an independent data audit of Toronto's shelter system, built on
public records and reproducible methods. Data and methodology inquiries:
[info@BonQuery.ca](mailto:info@BonQuery.ca).*
